title: Fiery Smoked Delight Croatian Salmon Gratin
description: A unique, vegan-friendly Croatian dish featuring smoked salmon, clay-pot-cooked to perfection in a raclette grill. The combination of smoky salmon, tangy lemon, and spicy peppers will transport your taste buds to the Dalmatian coast.

ingredients.name: Smoked salmon fillets
ingredients.notes: Look for wild-caught, sustainably sourced salmon

ingredients.name: Lemon wedges
ingredients.notes: Use fresh, organic lemons for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: Sweet or smoked paprika work well in this recipe

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Minced garlic adds depth to the dish without overpowering the salmon

ingredients.name: Red pepper flakes
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste, depending on your desired level of heat

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Use curly-leaf parsley for a traditional look

ingredients.name: Vegan cream cheese
ingredients.notes: This replaces traditional cream cheese in a vegan-friendly twist

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh dill
ingredients.notes: Dill adds a bright, fresh flavor to the dish

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use flaky sea salt for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper enhances the flavors of the dish

instructions: Rinse the smoked salmon fillets under cold water, pat them dry with paper towels, and set them aside.
In a small bowl, mix together the paprika, garlic, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper.
Place a piece of parchment paper on the raclette grill, leaving a small border around the edges.
Arrange the salmon fillets on the parchment paper, leaving a small gap between each fillet.
Sprinkle the paprika spice mixture evenly over the salmon fillets.
Place the raclette grill in the oven and cook for 8-10 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon cooks, mix together the vegan cream cheese, chopped parsley, and chopped dill in a small bowl.
Once the salmon is cooked, remove the raclette grill from the oven and carefully peel back the parchment paper.
Top each salmon fillet with a dollop of the cream cheese mixture and a sprinkle of lemon zest.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional parsley and lemon wedges if desired.
